subject: Rename white list to xfail list to clarify the role of the list
commit_message:
Rename white list to xfail list to clarify the role of the list

to be consistent with xfail lists introduced in security tests
by Pawel Wieczorek [1]

Issue-ID: INT-1435

[1]: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/103444

Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>
Change-Id: I5345607931e443f3335f34823c5cd80290425a45
Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

BTW I do not know how I could add dependencies to your patch to include it in the chain..

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> BTW I do not know how I could add dependencies to your patch to include it in the chain..

I did update list of required commands, but they're the same you used for JDWP. They are provided by packages: sed (sed) and coreutils (mktemp, cat) in Alpine as well as Ubuntu.

One can make sure they are installed by appending "sed coreutils" to https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/-/blob/master/security/docker/Dockerfile#L28 but they should be available by default.

I'm not sure if I got you right, though

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > BTW I do not know how I could add dependencies to your patch to include it in the chain..
> 
> I did update list of required commands, but they're the same you used for JDWP. They are provided by packages: sed (sed) and coreutils (mktemp, cat) in Alpine as well as Ubuntu.
> 
> One can make sure they are installed by appending "sed coreutils" to https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/-/blob/master/security/docker/Dockerfile#L28 but they should be available by default.
> 
> I'm not sure if I got you right, though

hehe no it was just a pure gerrit question
I am not used to create dependencies between gerrit reviews.
In this case my new patch could have been set as a dependency to your change so we would merger erything at once
just my lack of gerrit knowledge

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2:
> > > 
> > > BTW I do not know how I could add dependencies to your patch to include it in the chain..
> > 
> > I did update list of required commands, but they're the same you used for JDWP. They are provided by packages: sed (sed) and coreutils (mktemp, cat) in Alpine as well as Ubuntu.
> > 
> > One can make sure they are installed by appending "sed coreutils" to https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/-/blob/master/security/docker/Dockerfile#L28 but they should be available by default.
> > 
> > I'm not sure if I got you right, though
> 
> hehe no it was just a pure gerrit question
> I am not used to create dependencies between gerrit reviews.
> In this case my new patch could have been set as a dependency to your change so we would merger erything at once
> just my lack of gerrit knowledge

To be honest the only way to manage dependencies between patches in Gerrit I have used so far was downloading corresponding patchsets to local working tree and rebasing directly on them.

Adding Change-Id of corresponding patch to e.g. commit message will create a link in Gerrit web view but no merge dependencies (as far as I know )

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2:
> > > 
> > > BTW I do not know how I could add dependencies to your patch to include it in the chain..
> > 
> > I did update list of required commands, but they're the same you used for JDWP. They are provided by packages: sed (sed) and coreutils (mktemp, cat) in Alpine as well as Ubuntu.
> > 
> > One can make sure they are installed by appending "sed coreutils" to https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/integration/xtesting/-/blob/master/security/docker/Dockerfile#L28 but they should be available by default.
> > 
> > I'm not sure if I got you right, though
> 
> hehe no it was just a pure gerrit question
> I am not used to create dependencies between gerrit reviews.
> In this case my new patch could have been set as a dependency to your change so we would merger erything at once
> just my lack of gerrit knowledge

This can be achieved by either like Pawel says (checkout commit you'd like to depend on, then create your commit in local clone and push - Gerrit will put it in relation chain) or in GErrit UI by "Rebase" -> "Rebase on a specific change, ref, or commit"
Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as c27713e8c8b5467e8e900f7020227de366c67014 by Bartek Grzybowski
